mapping.a_element: adaptive bias potential to explore collective variables
mapping.b_element: bias potential constructed from locally valid collective coordinates

mapping.a_element: collective variables as order parameters for free energy landscape
mapping.b_element: collective coordinates derived from trajectory analyses

mapping.a_element: tunable parameters to focus computational effort on physically relevant regions
mapping.b_element: adaptive collective coordinates that adjust to new features during simulation

mapping.a_element: unified framework with metadynamics as a limiting case
mapping.b_element: methodology that enhances sampling in molecular dynamics (metadynamics-inspired)

brief_justification: Both methods employ an adaptive bias potential to explore collective variables/coordinates in molecular dynamics, aiming to enhance sampling and reconstruct free energy landscapes. The core machinery involves dynamically adjusting the bias to focus on relevant regions of the order parameter space, with metadynamics as a shared conceptual foundation.
